questions: How do I log in to ComplyWhistle?
How do I sign in?
How do I access the platform?
How do I enter ComplyWhistle?
answer: Open ComplyWhistle in a web browser, use the Login option, then click Sign In. If your organization uses SSO, continue through your identity provider. If a welcome or consent screen appears, click Continue.

questions: What can I do from the home page?
What is on the dashboard?
Where do I start in ComplyWhistle?
How do I start using the platform?
answer: Start from the main dashboard or AML area, where you can open deeds, review Persons of Interest, monitor statuses such as Pending, Approved, or Findings, and open available actions for follow-up.

questions: How do I create an AML Deed?
How do I create a deed?
How do I start a new AML case?
How do I open a new AML matter?
answer: Go to AML → Deeds, click Create AML Deed, complete the mandatory fields including title, description, and image, then click Create Deed.

questions: What fields do I need to create an AML Deed?
What information is required for a deed?
Why can I not create the deed yet?
What is mandatory when creating a deed?
answer: An AML Deed normally requires the core deed details to be completed before it can be created, including the title, description, and image field used by the workflow.

questions: Why do I need an image for the deed?
What image should I upload?
Why is the image field required?
Why can my image not be uploaded?
answer: The deed workflow expects an image to be attached as part of the record. If upload fails, try a smaller JPG or PNG file, confirm the file is not corrupted, and retry.

questions: How do I add a person of interest?
How do I add a POI?
How do I add a buyer or seller?
How do I add another person to the deed?
answer: In the deed card, go to the Persons of Interest section, enter the role label such as buyer, seller, beneficiary, or director, then click the plus button to add the POI.

questions: What is the role label for a POI?
What role should I choose for the person?
How do I classify the POI?
What roles can a POI have?
answer: The role label describes the POI's relationship to the deed, for example buyer, seller, beneficiary, director, or another role your organization uses in its process.

questions: Can I add more than one POI?
How do I add multiple POIs?
Can one deed have several persons of interest?
Can I add another buyer or director?
answer: Yes. Add each Person of Interest separately inside the same deed so each person can receive and complete their own screening journey and status can be tracked individually.

questions: How do I send the POI link?
How do I copy the screening link?
How do I send the digital journey link?
Where do I find the link for the POI?
answer: Open the relevant deed, find the POI, click Copy link, then paste the copied URL into an email, WhatsApp message, or SMS and send it to the person of interest.

questions: How do I resend the POI link?
Can I send the link again?
How do I resend the screening URL?
The person lost the link. What do I do?
answer: Open the same deed, locate the POI, copy the link again, and resend it through the preferred channel. The operator can usually reuse the copied URL for the same pending journey.

questions: What should the POI do after receiving the link?
What happens after I send the link?
How does the person complete the journey?
What does the client do with the link?
answer: After receiving the copied link, the Person of Interest should open it in a supported browser and complete the digital journey by providing the requested information, documents, or verification steps.

questions: What does Pending mean?
Why is the POI still pending?
What does Approved mean?
What does Findings mean?
answer: Pending means the screening journey is not yet completed or the result has not synced back yet. Approved means no findings were returned. Findings means one or more hits were returned and follow-up actions may be required.

questions: The POI finished but it still says Pending. Why?
Why is it still pending after completion?
The journey is done but the status did not change.
Why did the result not sync back yet?
answer: If the person already completed the journey and the status is still Pending, refresh the page first and re-open the deed. If it still does not change, it may mean the result has not synced back yet or the journey was not fully completed.

questions: What kind of findings can appear?
What checks are performed?
What can trigger a finding?
What types of hits can be returned?
answer: Depending on the configured workflow, findings can relate to screening hits such as PEP, sanctions, adverse media, or other configured review outcomes that require operator follow-up.

questions: What do I do when there are Findings?
How do I handle a finding?
What happens if there is a PEP hit?
What should I do with a screening hit?
answer: Review the case, open the available actions, and use the reporting and evidence tools as required. This may include viewing details, downloading the FIU report package, downloading the PDF report, downloading proof images as ZIP, or sending the FIU report by email depending on the case and your procedures.

questions: What actions are available after a finding?
What can I do from the findings screen?
Which buttons should I use after a hit?
What follow-up actions exist in the case?
answer: After a finding, the available actions usually focus on review and reporting, such as opening details, preparing or downloading report files, exporting supporting proof, and sending the report by email where enabled.

questions: What is the FIU report package?
What does download FIU package do?
What is included in the FIU report file?
Why would I download the FIU package?
answer: The FIU report package is the case-related export prepared for reporting and documentation purposes. It is intended to help the operator collect the relevant report information and supporting material for follow-up.

questions: What is the PDF report?
How do I download the PDF report?
What is the purpose of the PDF file?
Why would I export a PDF report?
answer: The PDF report provides a document version of the case or review output that can be saved, shared internally, or kept in the file as supporting documentation.

questions: What are proof images as ZIP?
How do I download proof images?
Why does it export a ZIP file?
What is inside the proof ZIP?
answer: The proof-images ZIP export is used to download supporting images or evidence files related to the case in one compressed package for review, archiving, or escalation.

questions: How do I send the FIU report by email?
Can I email the report from ComplyWhistle?
What does send FIU report do?
How do I email the case report?
answer: If the action is available in your environment, open the case actions and use the FIU email option to send the prepared report output. If the option is not visible, your role or environment may not allow it.

questions: What does Silence notifications do?
How do reminder emails work?
How do I stop reminders?
Why am I getting reminder emails?
answer: If a POI has Findings and has not yet been reported, reminder emails are sent automatically. Silence notifications stops those reminder emails for that specific POI.

questions: What are objective and subjective indicators?
How are indicators used in reporting?
What does indicator mean in the case?
Why does the case mention indicators?
answer: Indicators help the operator assess and document why a case may require reporting or further review. Use the case details together with your organization's procedures to determine the appropriate follow-up.

questions: Why can I not see a button?
Why do I not have the same actions as another user?
Why is an action missing?
Do permissions affect what I can do?
answer: Yes. What you can view or do may depend on your role, permissions, or environment configuration. If an expected action is missing, check with your administrator or AXIOMA.

questions: Which browser should I use?
Does the link work in every browser?
What browser is recommended?
Which browser should the POI use?
answer: Use a modern supported browser. If a copied link or journey does not behave correctly, retry in a current browser and, where possible, test again in Chrome.

questions: What if the link does not open?
What if the POI stays pending?
Why can I not upload the image?
How do I troubleshoot a problem in ComplyWhistle?
answer: If the POI stays Pending, confirm the journey was completed and refresh the page. If the link does not open, resend the copied link via a different channel and confirm the POI is using a supported browser. If the image does not upload, try a smaller JPG or PNG file and retry.

questions: How do I search for a deed?
Can I search deeds by name?
What does the Search box on AML Deeds do?
How do I find an existing deed?
answer: Use the Search field on the AML Deeds page to look up existing deed cards. Type part of the deed title, reference, or another identifying term to narrow the list.

questions: How do I filter deeds by status?
What does the Status filter do on AML Deeds?
Can I show only deeds with findings?
How do I filter the deed list?
answer: Use the Status dropdown on the AML Deeds page to filter the list of deeds. Select a status such as All or another available status to focus on the deeds you want to review.

questions: What does the three-dot menu on a deed do?
Where are the deed actions?
How do I open more options for a deed?
What is in the deed options menu?
answer: Open the three-dot menu on the deed card to access additional deed-level actions such as editing the deed, archiving it, sharing it by email, or downloading the deed package if those options are available in your environment.

questions: How do I edit a deed?
Can I change deed details after creating it?
What does Edit deed do?
How do I update a deed title or description?
answer: Open the deed's three-dot menu and choose Edit deed to update the deed information. Use this when you need to correct or revise the deed details after creation.

questions: How do I archive a deed?
What does Archive do on a deed?
Can I remove an old deed from active view?
How do I archive an AML Deed?
answer: Open the deed's three-dot menu and choose Archive when you want to move the deed out of your active working list in line with your team's process.

questions: How do I share a deed by email?
What does Share deed email do?
Can I email a deed to someone?
How do I send deed details by email?
answer: Open the deed's three-dot menu and choose Share deed (email) to send the deed through the email-sharing option available for that record.

questions: How do I download the deed package?
What does Download deed package ZIP do?
Can I export the deed as a ZIP file?
What is in the deed package?
answer: Open the deed's three-dot menu and choose Download deed package (ZIP) to export the deed-related package in a compressed ZIP file for sharing, filing, or review.

questions: How do I add an attachment to a deed?
What is the Attachments section on a deed?
How do I upload a file to the deed?
What does Add file do on a deed?
answer: Use the Attachments section on the deed card and click Add file to upload supporting documents or other files linked to that deed.
